enrichment.summary: Kyle recaps his October 27, 2016 day in Boulder: commuting frustrations, having Whopper meals with his mom, filling out his ballot (voting Hillary Clinton, straight Democrat, Green Party where available, and for various tax increases), getting set up on Carbonite backup, and working on a Google Earth project cataloging defunct ski resorts. He ends noting his internet is down and he may not be able to upload the video.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Voted for Hillary Clinton; does not want Donald Trump to be president
Votes Democrat across the line, with Green Party as secondary preference
Voted in favor of increasing them

stances.topic: making it harder to amend the Constitution

stances.position: Thinks it's a good deal and wishes he'd had it earlier when losing Google Earth data

transcript.text: Hey, living in Boulder V-LOG for October 27th, 2016. So, what did I do today? I went to work today. I felt like an idiot though because I drove.
So, I thought it was going to be too cold, but it wasn't too cold. And I got stuck in traffic on the way. I don't feel like an idiot.
But yeah, so then I come home and I'm working. And my mom calls me. She goes, I'm at Burger King right now. Come on over now. I have two burger meals.
So, she goes, drive. So, I was going to drive because it was going to be fast or something. So, I drove and I got stuck in traffic and I took forever. Pissed me off.
But yeah, she got two burger meals, Big Mac meals because or Whopper meals because somehow she came across some coupon that gave two for one or something.
So, you know, we had that. And then, oh, and then I went to her house and we filled out the ballot and I voted for all this stuff.
I voted for Hillary Clinton because I don't want Donald Trump to be president. And then I voted just Democrat across the line.
And then Green Party when there's a Green Party. And then I voted to increase the taxes on sugar and I think the tobacco tax and the other taxes and stuff.
So, yeah. But I voted against making it harder to put amendments on the Constitution.
So, then, but I didn't do any housework. And that's all I can think about that I did.
And then, oh, and then I got set up on Carbonite. My mom wanted to get me on Carbonite. So, I got on Carbonite. Unlimited Carbonite.
It's only $120 for three years. Unlimited Carbonite. So, yeah, that's cool. I'm going to check in on that.
Figure out how that works. I do have a MacBook or whatever, Apple Cloud. I haven't figured that out. I'm going to figure that out too.
But, yeah, if I had had that before I started losing all that stuff on Google Earth, before I figured out how to avoid losing stuff.
I think I even lost some stuff recently. So, yeah, I would have been better off because you can do a time machine backup.
But, and then I came back here and I worked some more on ski resorts.
He was doing old ski resorts, defunct ski resorts today. They're smaller. They're all in a smaller dimension.
So, there's more of them. There's 63 of them as opposed to 28. But you go through faster.
I don't have internet right now. The guy came and gave me a new modem the other day, but that didn't fix anything.
I just, that's why I'm doing the video now. I just tried to watch, like, I burnt out on my work and I just tried to watch some Young Turks videos.
And I can't get on YouTube. I can't get on YouTube at all. So, that means, I don't know, maybe I won't even be able to upload this.
So, I guess I'll just, I'll just hang up, hang on to this and, um, until I can upload it.
You know, I won't upload anymore until I, and then I'll call tomorrow and I'll tell them what's going on and maybe they can figure something out.
So, I guess that's it for today's vlog. Four minutes. All right. Peace.
